---
title: Includes
---

Includes let you insert reusable content into any document. They are useful when you find yourself copying the same block of content -- a callout, a disclaimer, a standard methods section -- across multiple pages. Instead of duplicating the text, write it once and reference it by name.


# Syntax

Use the standard Jinja `include` tag. When the path has a file extension, it resolves relative to the project root:

{% raw %}
```
{% include "path/to/file.qmd" %}
```
{% endraw %}

When the path has **no extension**, *Calepin* uses format-aware resolution under `_calepin/partials/`:

{% raw %}
```
{% include "source_tip" %}
```
{% endraw %}


# Where to store reusable content

For format-aware includes (no extension), files live in `_calepin/partials/`, organized by format:

```
_calepin/
  partials/
    common/          # format-agnostic (.jinja extension)
    html/            # HTML-specific (.html extension)
    latex/           # LaTeX-specific (.tex extension)
    typst/           # Typst-specific (.typ extension)
```


# Resolution order

When *Calepin* encounters {% raw %}`{% include "source_tip" %}`{% endraw %} (no extension), it looks for a file in this order (first match wins):

1. **Target-specific** -- `_calepin/partials/{target}/source_tip.{ext}` (e.g., `_calepin/partials/website/source_tip.html` when rendering the `website` target)
2. **Engine-specific** -- `_calepin/partials/{engine}/source_tip.{ext}` (e.g., `_calepin/partials/html/source_tip.html`)
3. **Format-agnostic** -- `_calepin/partials/common/source_tip.jinja`

Most reusable content only needs a single file in `common/`. Use the format-specific directories when the content must differ between output formats -- for example, when it contains raw HTML or LaTeX.


# Examples

## A reusable callout

File `_calepin/partials/common/source_tip.jinja`:

{% raw %}
````
::: {.callout-tip}
::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
Click the **</>** button in the menu at the top of this page to see the `.qmd` source code for this example.
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="latex"}
The source code for this page is available at: <https://vincentarelbundock.github.io/calepin>
:::
:::
````
{% endraw %}

Then in any document:

{% raw %}
```
{% include "source_tip" %}
```
{% endraw %}

## A standard disclaimer with metadata

Included content goes through normal Jinja processing after expansion, so it can reference front matter fields and variables:

File `_calepin/partials/common/draft_notice.jinja`:

{% raw %}
````
::: {.callout-warning}
## Draft

This is a draft of *{{ meta.title }}*. Do not cite or circulate.
:::
````
{% endraw %}

## Format-specific content

When the same include needs fundamentally different markup per format, use the format-specific directories. For example, to embed a custom HTML widget in HTML output and a static image in LaTeX:

File `_calepin/partials/html/widget.html`:

```html
<div id="interactive-widget">...</div>
```

File `_calepin/partials/latex/widget.tex`:

```latex
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{fig/widget-static.pdf}
```

Both are accessed with the same call: {% raw %}`{% include "widget" %}`{% endraw %}.
